exercises.title: SIGNAL FORMAT
promptLines: A `string | number` arrives. Define **render(x: string | number): string**. If `x` is a number return `'#' + x`; if it is a string return it **uppercased**. Use a `typeof` guard to narrow the union.
exercises.hint: if (typeof x === 'number') return '#' + x; else return x.toUpperCase();
exercises.lore: Narrow the beam before you trust the packet.

exercises.title: PAYLOAD SIZE
promptLines: Define **sizeOf(x: string | unknown[]): number** returning its `.length`. Both a string and an array expose `.length`, so the union resolves to one expression.
exercises.lore: Same ruler measures every signal on the wire.

exercises.title: GAIT COUNT
exercises.brief: Count limbs off a tagged union.
promptLines: A **discriminated union** tags each member with `kind`. Define **legsOf(a: { kind: 'dog'; legs: number } | { kind: 'snake' }): number**.
Switch on `a.kind`: a `'dog'` returns `a.legs`; a `'snake'` returns `0`.
starterLines: function legsOf(a: { kind: 'dog'; legs: number } | { kind: 'snake' }): number {
solutionLines: function legsOf(a: { kind: 'dog'; legs: number } | { kind: 'snake' }): number {
exercises.lore: The tag tells you which creature crawled onto the grid.

exercises.title: ID RESOLVER
exercises.brief: A bare id or a wrapper carrying one.
promptLines: Define **idText(x: number | { id: number }): string** returning `'ID:'` followed by the id. If `x` is a number use it directly; otherwise read `x.id`. Narrow with a `typeof` guard.
starterLines: function idText(x: number | { id: number }): string {
// TODO: narrow then read the id
solutionLines: function idText(x: number | { id: number }): string {
exercises.hint: if (typeof x === 'number') return 'ID:' + x; else return 'ID:' + x.id;
exercises.lore: Some records hand you the key; others hide it in a struct.

exercises.title: LIST COERCE
promptLines: Define a generic **toArray<T>(x: T | T[]): T[]**. If `x` is already an array, pass it through unchanged; otherwise wrap the lone value in `[x]`. Use an `Array.isArray` guard to narrow.
starterLines: // TODO: wrap a value, pass an array through
exercises.lore: One or many — the buffer takes them both the same way.
